title: dryopteris inaequalis
intro: Dryopteris inaequalis, also known as the Inequilateral Shield Fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Dryopteris inaequalis can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are typically consumed orally or applied topically under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
